parsed_review.summary: CP00-846 is a descriptor-only RP28 Marketplace pack extending the domain model through the ConnectorSDK tail, CustomAIApp slice, and ReviewGate head (implemented_model_names grows to MarketplaceApp/ConnectorSDK/CustomAIApp/ReviewGate) without opening any marketplace runtime. Read-only verification confirms the pack-level production_ready claim is honestly NOT yet asserted: manifest declares production_ready=false, runtime_ready=false, implementation_layer=descriptor, status=in_progress, pending exactly this gating review plus full validation. Implementation (model.js/states.js/registry.js/validators.js) is frozen descriptor data; validators actively reject any row asserting runtime execution, connector runtime load, custom-AI execution, install-receipt emission, or permission/audit/product-state writes, and reject embedded connector secrets, OAuth tokens, prompt/completion payloads, real client data, permission decision detail, or audit bodies. I directly counted the live closeout-pack-plan.json CP00-846 block (40 units; deliverable distribution implementation:23/ui:5/fixture:2/test:6/hermes_evidence:2/claude_review:2; micro-title Type-And-Shape:12/Primary:22/Secondary:6), matching the binding and MARKETPLACE_CP846_REQUIREMENTS, so coverage/descriptor/contract validators are internally consistent. The contract projection (latest_pack CP00-846, latest_projection MarketplaceCp846DomainCustomAiReviewGateDescriptor, validation.valid=true, complete no_write_attestation) is consistent; all referenced npm scripts exist; no secrets or real data found in the package. Authority boundary holds: claude_is_final_approval=false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false, read_only packets, human approval required before runtime opening; Hermes packets emit_runtime_receipt=false (deterministic gate handling, not product runtime). The pending claude-review-result.json placeholder (0-byte stdout, status pending) is correctly NOT counted as a valid review (exactly_one_valid_pack_level_claude_review_run=false; auth preflight passed), so no invalid/auth-failed/malformed evidence is being passed off as valid. No P0/P1 issues; two P3 nits noted. blocks_goal_closeout=false: this pack claims neither runtime nor enterprise trust, and Claude's non-final-approver status is the expected boundary, not a defect.

findings.title: Full closeout validation suite not executed within read-only review scope
findings.details: command-evidence.json records only two preliminary commands (npm run rp28:marketplace:validate and node --test packages/marketplace/test/*.test.js), both exit_code 0, with status 'pending_full_validation' and gate_outcome 'pending_valid_review_and_full_validation'. The broader closeout suite expected before promotion (closeout-pack-plan:validate, closeout-pack:validate, npm test, validate, spec:requirements:validate, weighted:validate, fullplan:validate, goal:closeout:validate, rp00:control-plane:validate, git diff --check) is not yet evidenced. As a read-only reviewer (Read/Grep/Glob, no Bash) I verified code/contract/test/plan consistency by static inspection only and cannot execute these commands; their passing is still a prerequisite for production_ready.
findings.recommendation: Orchestrator must run and capture the full validation command suite at exit 0 before flipping production_ready=true and committing. The static review confirms structural soundness but does not substitute for executing the suite.
findings.post_review_disposition: Expected workflow sequencing, not a code defect; production_ready is correctly still false and gate_outcome already records pending_full_validation.

findings.title: Manifest no_write_attestation omits app_update_review_executed flag present elsewhere
findings.details: manifest.no_write_attestation lists 15 guard flags but omits app_update_review_executed, which is present (=false) in packages/marketplace/src/registry.js MARKETPLACE_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION and in contracts/marketplace-custom-ai-apps-contract.json validation.no_write_attestation. The app-update-review guard is still enforced via per-row createRow attestations and the validators' runtime-open check, so the no-write boundary itself is intact; only the manifest summary is incomplete.
findings.recommendation: Add app_update_review_executed:false to manifest no_write_attestation for parity with the registry and contract attestations.
findings.post_review_disposition: Cosmetic consistency nit; does not weaken the no-write/no-real-data boundary since the authoritative attestations checked by validators are complete.
